name: Harvard Square — Winter 1969
description: Harvard Square, Cambridge MA, mid-January 1969. Late-60s counter-culture epicenter a week before Nixon's inauguration. Traffic on Mass Ave (V8 sedans, VW Beetles, diesel MBTA buses), students crossing brick sidewalks in winter boots, church bells from Memorial Church, protest chants drifting from the Yard, shop doors releasing bursts of radio and warmth. Red Line subway rumbles underfoot. Listener at 2nd-floor apartment above a shop, window cracked against the cold. Faint steam radiator hiss and occasional pipe clank from inside the room.

assetGeneration.promptContext: Immersive winter soundscape of Harvard Square, Cambridge MA in January 1969. Brick-and-mortar university town, narrow streets between 3-story brick buildings. Late-60s American cars (V8 sedans, VW Beetles), diesel city buses, brick sidewalks, bare winter trees, light snow. Anti-war student activism, bookshops, coffeehouses. No modern hybrids, no cell phones, no digital sounds.

newspaper_vendor.description: Middle-aged male, gruff Boston accent, outdoor voice projecting over traffic

protester.description: Young male, 18-22 years old, passionate and rhythmic, slight New England accent, chanting in unison cadence

student_female.description: Young female, 19-21 years old, casual conversational tone, educated New England accent

sources.generatedPrompt: Late 1960s era, circa 1969. Sound effect: Checker cab idling at the curb — big inline-six engine rumbling at idle, passenger door slam (heavy metal door), cab pulls away. Surface material: asphalt.
